user.title: Timeline Scheduler
user.description: Add Timeline Scheduler? This registers the scheduling engine, its services, and the timeline card. No configuration is required.

abort.single_instance_allowed: Timeline Scheduler is already set up; only one instance is supported.

user.title: Add schedule
user.description: Create a schedule. You'll lay out its timeline of setpoints afterward in the Timeline Scheduler card.

data_description.target: The entity this schedule controls — a switch/light, a climate (thermostat), or a number.
data_description.apply:
How each setpoint value is sent to the target — match it to your target entity:

- **Switch on/off** — a switch or light. Values are on/off.
- **Climate temperature** — a thermostat. Values are temperatures, e.g. 72.
- **Climate HVAC mode** — a thermostat's mode. Values like heat, cool, off.
- **Number value** — a number entity. Values are plain numbers.

reconfigure.title: Edit schedule
reconfigure.description: Change this schedule's name, target, or how its values are applied. Edit the timeline itself in the card.

data_description.target: The entity this schedule controls — a switch/light, a climate (thermostat), or a number.
data_description.apply:
How each setpoint value is sent to the target — match it to your target entity:

- **Switch on/off** — a switch or light. Values are on/off.
- **Climate temperature** — a thermostat. Values are temperatures, e.g. 72.
- **Climate HVAC mode** — a thermostat's mode. Values like heat, cool, off.
- **Number value** — a number entity. Values are plain numbers.

abort.entry_not_loaded: Timeline Scheduler isn't loaded. Restart Home Assistant or re-add the integration, then try again.
